title: hieronyma oblonga var. genuina
intro: Hieronyma oblonga var. genuina, also known as the Mexican oak tree or Guanacaste tree, is a tropical tree native to Central America and Mexico. The tree is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Hieronyma oblonga var. genuina are attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots, which are used to prepare herbal remedies that provide relief from pain and inflammation, as well as to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Hieronyma oblonga var. genuina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the bark, leaves, or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
